Aequor Realm <:realm_aequor:1437283645051375777> 
A realm unique among all others for the power level of its cards relative to its mechanic
What does Blue give you?
1) stats Units like Caecus and Goliath have some of the highest stat totals in the game despite being Standard units, which directly correlate to high hp comps
2) Tentacles, the weakest realm mechanic but it possesses its own unique strengths, partly strength, gaining practically double the advantage from stacking str but also nice to have passive dmg and free shields.
3) CARD AND UNIT QUALITY, Aequor as a realm has the highest card quality with NEARLY no dead cards per unit and the ones who do having a value that overrides it. Compare that to a lot of other realms whose units, by and large, literally exist to sit there and never play cards.
Now all this to say Blue has its disadvantages as well
1) Realm mixing. Other realms don't really lose anything by mixing, rather Caro Ultra is the meta for the community, but Aequor hurts. Not to be that you cannot mix it with Caro/Ultra just that a bit of the raw str is lost so the power of those units are important
2) DPS, as a realm Aequor lacks the most in effective DPS units. In the realm itself its just Murphy: Fauxborn at "normal" investments (overexalt). Everyone else is even more expensive/restrictive.
That is why Chaos is ur best friend. Fills in the missing spaces, namely dps units.
Teams (Tier 1): These ones take advantage of being Aequor but necessitate overexalt dps for full power
Tawil - Gets the best card pool, great dmg with the tentacles, and a consitent vuln source with caecus as well as being flexible
24 - Gains the most from being Blue becomes an amazing poison dps but needs strong def like Celeste and Murphy
Murphy: Fauxborn - Can be played by herself or with Lemurians to dominate all content
expected this after i finished watching a 10 turn corpo aurita play and there was still typing

Teams (Tier 2): Either weaker teams or just doesn't take as much advantage
Corporita - AKA the discard comp, relies on Corposant burst and Aurita's cycling with a few various units that synergize
Sanga - a comp that's long fallen out of favor that relies on Myriam, Winkle, Horla with OE Sanga to stack shields and dmg from shields and exalting every turn
Kath/Mouchette - Just normal Kath/Mouchette gameplay but w/ Celeste just cuz
For more information or further inquiries ping <@285755067213152257>

Why don't we count <:wisdom:1400771656510734356> 
Strike (1 hit) - +1
Mortal Blast (1 hit) - +1
Shining ⭐ Crush (5 hits) - +1
Pursuit (3 hits x4) - +4
19 hits in total vs 26 hits with E3, assuming you're only using each of her cards once, which is a ~27% increase in terms of benefiting from the flat damage bonus

What are the pros and cons of a Pollux team vs monowil? I know Tawil is getting a lot of hate lately but I can see she's still being used very often to clear wave 5 in d zone
Pollux does twice as much dmg with about 5x fewer cards per turn

they are both single target dps that need high investment

pollux:
- much higher damage at same investment
- applies a lot of bleed which can skip mechanics
- doesnt do much other than damage

tawil:
- can discover a lot of cards and gains access to utility options from 6 wings
- multihits a lot (can be good or bad)
- does str down as part of their combo
- notoriously time consuming, finicky, hard to play
